name: Puerto de Arteta and Alto de Ulzurrun Loop from Pamplona
name_original: Puerto de Arteta y Alto de Ulzurrun desde Pamplona
description: This moderate 37.7-mile road cycling route from Pamplona climbs to the Port of Arteta and High of Ulzurrun, offering scenic Pyrenean views.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Pampelune traverse a diverse landscape, ranging from lush urban green spaces to the foothills of the Pyrenees. The region features varied terrains, including verdant valleys and areas bordering semi-arid deserts. Cyclists can find routes that span from flat sections along river paths to more demanding climbs in the surrounding mountains. This strategic location in Navarre, Spain, offers a well-developed network for road cyclists. Best road cycling routes around Pampelune The most popular road cycling route is Pamplona - Viana via the EuroVelo 3, a 99.9 miles (160.8 km) trail that takes 7 hours 39 minutes to complete. This difficult route follows a segment of the EuroVelo network, offering a long-distance cycling experience. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Port of Arteta and High of Ulzurrun from Pamplona, a moderate 37.7 miles (60.6 km) path. This route includes significant elevation gain, providing challenging climbs through the surrounding hills. Local road cyclists also love the Monte Ezkaba – tour around Ultzama through Ostiz and Marcalain, a 39.9 miles (64.2 km) trail leading through varied terrain around Monte Ezkaba, often completed in about 3 hours 15 minutes. Road cycling around Pampelune is defined by the foothills of the Pyrenees, verdant valleys, and river paths, offering diverse scenery. The network provides options for various ability levels, from easy urban rides to challenging mountain ascents. The routes in Pampelune are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 300 reviews. More than 14,000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Pampelune's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Pampelune?
A: There are over 140 road cycling routes around Pampelune documented on komoot. These routes cater to various skill levels, with approximately 9 easy, 66 moderate, and 72 difficult options, ensuring a suitable ride for every cyclist.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when road cycling near Pampelune?
A: The region around Pampelune offers a diverse range of terrain. You can find flat sections along river paths like the Parque Fluvial del Arga, rolling hills, and more demanding climbs in the foothills of the Pyrenees. Landscapes vary from verdant valleys to areas bordering semi-arid deserts like Bardenas Reales, providing a rich and varied cycling experience.

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes for experienced cyclists?
A: Yes, Pampelune offers numerous challenging routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984904446/pamplona-viana-via-the-eurovelo-3" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pamplona - Viana via the EuroVelo 3&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult, long-distance route with over 2,100 meters of elevation gain. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31972862" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de Erro Ascent – Rabia Bridge, Zubiri loop from Pamplona/Iruña&lt;/a&gt;, which features significant climbs.

Q: What are some easier road cycling routes suitable for beginners or a relaxed ride?
A: For an easier ride, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e986759580/tour-around-pamplona" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tour around Pamplona&lt;/a&gt;, which is an easy 25 km loop with minimal elevation gain. The city's "Paseo Fluvial" also offers a network of cycling paths along the rivers, providing a gentle introduction to cycling in the area.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Pampelune?
A: The climate in Pampelune is generally favorable for cycling almost year-round. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and beautiful scenery. Summer can be warm, especially in the more exposed areas, while winter might bring cooler temperatures, but many routes remain accessible.

Q: Can I find family-friendly road cycling options around Pampelune?
A: Yes, the region has options suitable for families. The "Voies Vertes" (Greenways), such as the Via Verde de Plazaola, are built on former railway lines, providing easy, safe, and scenic paths away from traffic, ideal for all ages and fitness levels. The urban green spaces and river paths also offer gentle rides.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes around Pampelune?
A: Many routes around Pampelune are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e986759580/tour-around-pamplona" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tour around Pamplona&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example of a circular route. You can also find loops that incorporate climbs like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e986649027/port-of-arteta-and-high-of-ulzurrun-from-pamplona" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Port of Arteta and High of Ulzurrun from Pamplona&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What interesting landmarks or natural features can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: Road cycling routes around Pampelune can lead you past a variety of attractions. You might encounter historical sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4396525" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pamplona Cathedral and the Citadel&lt;/a&gt;, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4817230" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Magdalena Bridge&lt;/a&gt;. Natural features include the lush trails of Urbasa Andía Natural Park, the striking semi-desert of Bardenas Reales, and the beautiful Irati Forest. Some routes offer views from points like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3690724" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View from Alto del Perdón&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there routes that follow historical paths like the Camino de Santiago?
A: Yes, Pampelune is a key stop on the historic Camino de Santiago (French Way), which can be traversed by bicycle. This route offers a unique cultural experience, passing through medieval towns and varied scenery. For more information on starting the Camino in Navarre, you can visit &lt;a href="https://www.visitnavarra.es/fr/-/5-itin%C3%A9raires-pour-commencer-le-chemin-de-compostelle-en-navarre" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;visitnavarra.es&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Pampelune?
A: The road cycling routes around Pampelune are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 300 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse terrain, ranging from challenging mountain ascents to scenic river paths, and the well-developed network of routes that cater to all abilities.

Q: Are there any long-distance cycling routes passing through Pampelune?
A: Pampelune is part of the EuroVelo 1 Atlantic Coast Route, a long-distance cycling path that is part of a larger European network. This provides developed routes for avid cyclists looking for multi-day adventures. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984904446/pamplona-viana-via-the-eurovelo-3" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pamplona - Viana via the EuroVelo 3&lt;/a&gt; is an example of a segment of this network.

Q: Can I access road cycling routes using public transport in Pampelune?
A: Pamplona has a well-developed public transport system, and many routes are accessible from within or near the city center. The "Paseo Fluvial" and other urban routes are easily reached. For routes further afield, local bus services might offer connections, though specific bike carriage policies should be checked with the operator.

Q: Are there specific routes that offer scenic viewpoints?
A: Absolutely. The region is rich in natural beauty, and many routes offer stunning vistas. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3690724" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View from Alto del Perdón&lt;/a&gt; is a well-known viewpoint, offering panoramic views. Routes through the pre-Pyrenees and verdant valleys also provide numerous scenic spots to enjoy the landscape.
tips: Crowning its 833 meters above sea level is quite a challenge, but with impressive views throughout the journey from any direction it is taken. Some beautiful panoramic views of the entire Ollo Valley and towards the other side of Goñi.
Highway port at almost 850 masl with a very nice stretch of road between a dense grove of trees and with very nice views towards the Ollo Valley both coming from north or south.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this cycling route from Pamplona?
faqs.answer: The route starts directly from Pamplona, so you can typically find parking within the city. Many cyclists choose to park in public parking garages or designated street parking areas in Pamplona, then cycle to the route's starting point. There isn't a specific dedicated trailhead parking lot for this particular route outside the city.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: This route is designed for road cycling on paved surfaces. While dogs are generally allowed in public spaces in Spain, bringing a dog on a road cycling route of this nature is not typically recommended for their safety or yours due to traffic and the sustained pace. It's best suited for human cyclists.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle the Port of Arteta and High of Ulzurrun route?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle this route is generally during the spring (April to June) and autumn (September to October) months. During these periods, the weather in Navarre is typically mild and pleasant, with less extreme heat than summer and fewer chances of cold or wet conditions than winter. This makes for ideal cycling conditions through the Pyrenean foothills.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need any special permits or have to pay entrance fees to cycle this route. It utilizes public roads and passes through natural areas that are freely accessible to cyclists.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for beginner road cyclists?
faqs.answer: This route is categorized as moderate, covering approximately 60.6 kilometers with an elevation gain of about 830 meters. While the surfaces are well-paved, the climbs to the Port of Arteta and High of Ulzurrun require good fitness. It might be challenging for absolute beginners but is a rewarding experience for those with some road cycling experience and a decent fitness level.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The terrain on this route is predominantly well-paved roads, making it ideal for race bikes. You'll encounter a mix of ascents and descents as you traverse the Pyrenean foothills, with the main challenges being the climbs to the two mountain passes. The route is described as easy to ride in terms of surface quality.

faqs.question: What scenic views or points of interest can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: As you cycle through the Pyrenean foothills of Navarre, you can expect breathtaking views of diverse landscapes, including lush countryside and traditional Navarran villages. The climbs to the mountain passes, such as Puerto de Arteta (Alto de Goñi) and Ulzurrun Pass, offer expansive vistas of the surrounding natural beauty.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The estimated time to complete this 60.6-kilometer route is around 2 hours and 56 minutes. However, this can vary based on your fitness level, the number of stops you make for photos or refreshments, and weather conditions.

faqs.question: Are there places to stop for refreshments or amenities along the route?
faqs.answer: While the route itself focuses on the natural landscape and climbs, you will pass through or near several traditional Navarran villages. These villages often have small cafes, bars, or shops where you can stop for refreshments, water, or a quick snack. It's always a good idea to carry sufficient water and snacks, especially on longer climbs.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route intersect with or run alongside other local paths. For instance, you will briefly encounter parts of the Tour de la Valle de Ollo, the Tour of the Pamplona Basin, and The Pathway of the Arga river.

faqs.question: Are there any specific strategies for tackling the climbs on this route?
faqs.answer: The route features two main climbs to the Port of Arteta and High of Ulzurrun. Given its moderate difficulty, pacing yourself on the ascents is key. The roads are well-paved, so focus on maintaining a steady rhythm. Enjoy the scenery on the way up, and be prepared for rewarding descents with great views.
